name: Schwarzwalder Kirschtorte (Black Forest Cherry Cake)
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------SYRUP-----------------------------------
1/3 c Sugar, 2 tbsp Water
1/3 c Kirsch Liquor
----------------------------------FILLING----------------------------------
1 1/2 cups Confectioners' Sugar
1 Large Egg Yolk
1/3 cup Unsalted Butter
2 tbsp Kirsch Liquor
----------------------------------TOPPING----------------------------------
2 cups Canned Sour Cherries, drained
1 cup Heavy Cream, whipped
2 tbsp Confectioners' Sugar
8 oz Semisweet Chocolate Bar (1)
There should be 4 squares of chocolate and it should be melted.
CAKE:
1. Beat eggs, sugar, and vanilla together until thick and fluffy, about 10 minutes.
2. Alternately fold baking chocolate and flour into the egg mixture, ending with flour.
3. Pour batter into 3 greased and floured 8-inch cake pans.
4. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 10 to 15 minutes or until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean.
5. Cool cakes in pans for 5 minutes; turn out on racks to cool completely.
SYRUP:
1. Make syrup by mixing sugar and water, boiling for 5 minutes.
2. When syrup has cooled, stir in kirsch.
3. Prick cake layers and pour syrup over all 3 layers.
FILLING:
1. Beat together sugar and butter until well blended.
2. Add egg yolk; beat until light and fluffy, about 3 to 5 minutes.
3. Fold in Kirsch.
CAKE ASSEMBLY:
1. Place one layer on a cake plate.
2. Spread with butter-cream filling.
3. Drop cherries evenly over cream using about 3/4 cup of patted dry cherries.
4. Place second and third layers on top.
5. Fold 2 tbsp confectioners' sugar into whipped cream; spread sides and top of the cake.
6. Decorate top with remaining cherries.
7. Shave chocolate curls from chocolate bar at room temperature using a vegetable peeler.
8. Press chocolate curls onto the sides of the cake and sprinkle a few on the top.
9. Chill until serving time.
